questions.question: What geometric property is used to construct a line parallel to another?
questions.correct_answer: The property of equal alternate interior angles
questions.explanation: If a transversal intersects two lines such that alternate interior angles are equal, the lines are parallel.

questions.question: Which tools are primarily used for geometric constructions?
questions.explanation: A ruler is used for straight lines and a compass for circles and marking equal distances.

questions.question: What information is needed to construct a triangle using the SSS criterion?
questions.correct_answer: The lengths of all three sides
questions.explanation: Side-Side-Side construction requires knowing the length of every edge of the triangle.

questions.question: In SAS construction, where must the given angle be located?
questions.explanation: SAS stands for Side-Angle-Side; the angle must be 'included' by the sides.

questions.question: What does the RHS criterion stand for in triangle construction?
questions.explanation: This is used specifically for right-angled triangles.

questions.question: What is a 'tiling' or 'tessellation'?
questions.correct_answer: A pattern made of identical shapes that fits together without gaps or overlaps
questions.explanation: Tiles must cover the entire plane surface perfectly.

questions.question: Why can regular pentagons not tile a floor by themselves?
questions.correct_answer: Their interior angles do not sum to 360 degrees at a vertex
questions.explanation: For shapes to tile, the angles meeting at a point must total exactly 360 degrees.

questions.question: Which three regular polygons can create a regular tiling?
questions.correct_answer: Equilateral triangles, squares, and regular hexagons
questions.explanation: These are the only regular polygons whose interior angles are factors of 360.

questions.question: What is the first step in constructing a triangle with ASA given?
questions.correct_answer: Draw the given side length as the base
questions.explanation: The two angles are then constructed at either end of this base line.

questions.question: Where is tiling commonly seen in the real world?
questions.correct_answer: Floor patterns, honeycombs, and brick walls
questions.explanation: These are practical applications of fitting geometric shapes together.

notes.title: Alternate Angle Method
notes.content: To draw a parallel line, make an angle at a point on the line and copy it to the point outside the line.

notes.title: Triangle Inequality Rule
notes.content: A triangle can only be constructed if the sum of any two sides is greater than the third side.

notes.title: Protractor vs. Compass
notes.content: While a protractor measures angles, a compass is preferred for 'pure' geometric constructions of standard angles.

notes.title: Vertex Angle Sum
notes.content: In any tiling, the sum of angles where the corners of tiles meet must be 360°.

notes.title: Base Line Strategy
notes.content: Always start a construction by drawing the longest given side or the side associated with given angles.

facts.fact: A regular hexagon can tile a plane because its interior angle (120°) goes into 360° exactly three times.
Only one unique triangle can be drawn if SSS, SAS, ASA, or RHS criteria are met.
M.C. Escher is a famous artist known for creating complex and artistic tilings (tessellations).
The 'Hypotenuse' is the longest side and is always opposite the right angle.
